meta.name: Soy-Ginger Chicken
meta.subtitle: Savory-sweet chicken with ginger, garlic, and a glossy soy sauce finish.

source.name: SSA Canonical Catalog
notes: SSA canonical: weeknight-ready, batch-friendly, pairs with rice/noodles and quick greens.
Includes stove-top and oven fallback notes; supports thigh or breast.

summary: Tender chicken glazed in a soy-ginger-garlic sauce. Fast enough for a weeknight and perfect over rice with crisp-tender vegetables.

yield.notes: About 4 portions with rice and a vegetable side.

optional.label: Wok
optional.notes: Works well for quick sauce reduction and tossing with vegetables.

notes: Use tamari for gluten-free. Confirm any broth or chili sauce used is gluten-free if applicable.

ingredients.label: Chicken thighs, boneless skinless (or breasts)
notes: Thighs stay juicier. If using breast, avoid overcooking; see temp checks.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
notes: Use less if your soy sauce is very salty; adjust at the end.

ingredients.label: Cornstarch
notes: Used as a light coating for browning + a small amount for thickening sauce.

ingredients.label: Green onions, sliced (whites and greens separated)
Honey or brown sugar
notes: Honey gives shine; brown sugar gives deeper caramel notes.

ingredients.label: Rice vinegar
notes: Balances sweetness and lifts the soy.

ingredients.label: Toasted sesame oil
notes: Add at the end for aroma.

ingredients.label: Red pepper flakes or chili crisp (optional heat)
Optional: broccoli florets (quick one-pan add-in)
notes: If adding, steam-sauté after searing chicken using a splash of water, then toss into glaze.

steps.title: Prep and lightly coat the chicken
instructions: Pat chicken dry. Cut into bite-size pieces (about 1-inch).
Season with salt and black pepper.
Sprinkle 1 tbsp cornstarch over chicken and toss to coat lightly (this helps browning and thickens glaze).
checks: Chicken pieces should be evenly coated with a thin, dusty layer—no wet clumps.

steps.title: Mix the sauce
instructions: In a bowl, whisk soy sauce, honey/brown sugar, rice vinegar, and water/broth.
If you want a thicker glaze, whisk in remaining 1/2 tbsp cornstarch (optional for extra glossy sauce).
notes: If you prefer a lighter sauce, skip extra cornstarch and reduce longer in the pan.

steps.title: Sear the chicken
instructions: Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add neutral oil.
Add chicken in a single layer (work in batches if needed). Sear 2–3 minutes per side until browned.
Transfer browned chicken to a plate (it may not be fully cooked yet).
checks: Chicken should have browned edges; avoid overcrowding (steaming prevents browning).

steps.title: Optional: quick steam-sauté broccoli
instructions: If using broccoli: add broccoli florets to the pan and stir 30 seconds.
Add 2–3 tbsp water, cover, and steam 2 minutes.
Uncover and stir until water evaporates; transfer broccoli to the chicken plate.

steps.title: Glaze and finish
instructions: Lower heat to medium. Pour sauce into the pan and bring to a simmer.
Return chicken (and broccoli if using) to the pan and toss to coat.
Simmer 3–6 minutes until chicken is cooked through and sauce is glossy and lightly thickened.
Turn off heat. Stir in toasted sesame oil (if using).
Garnish with sliced green onion greens and sesame seeds. Add chili flakes/chili crisp if desired.
checks: Chicken doneness: 165°F (74°C) internal temperature (breast), 170–175°F (77–80°C) for thighs is ideal texture.
Sauce should cling to chicken; if too thick, add 1–2 tbsp water; if too thin, simmer 1–2 minutes longer.

remixIdeas: Slice and serve over noodles with extra green onions.
Turn into lettuce wraps with shredded carrots and cucumber.
Add to a rice bowl with quick pickled veg.

notes: Store in a shallow container for faster cooling.
Sauce thickens when cold; loosen when reheating with a splash of water.
Freeze in portions. Thaw overnight in the fridge for best texture.
Chicken breast can dry slightly after freezing; thighs hold up best.

reheat.skillet: Medium heat with 1–2 tbsp water; cover 1 minute, then stir until hot.
reheat.microwave: Cover; heat in 60-second bursts, stirring between.

notes: Cook chicken to safe internal temperature (165°F / 74°C minimum).
Cool leftovers quickly and refrigerate promptly.

variations.label: Orange Soy-Ginger Chicken
variations.notes: Replace 1/4 cup water/broth with orange juice and add 1 tsp orange zest. Reduce sugar slightly.

variations.label: Spicy Gochujang Twist
variations.notes: Add 1 tbsp gochujang to sauce; increase water/broth by 2 tbsp to keep it pourable.

variations.label: Crispy Tofu Version
variations.notes: Use extra-firm tofu cubes, coat with cornstarch, pan-fry until crisp, then glaze as directed.

notes: If sauce tastes too salty: add a squeeze of citrus or 1–2 tbsp water and a touch more honey.
If sauce tastes flat: add 1 tsp more vinegar or a tiny pinch of salt (only after tasting).
For extra ginger punch: add 1 tsp fresh grated ginger at the end off heat.
